original: SpaceXcompletes the first recapture of a rocket, as its Starship rocket booster is caught by a giant pair of mechanical arms in a test flight near Brownsville, Texas
Andy Green's Jet-powered car reaches record 749.69 MPH
NASA launches a spacecraft to explore the metal-rich asteroid Psyche - the largest metallic object in the Solar System
Copiapó mining accident in Chile comes to a happy end as all 33 miners arrive at the surface after surviving a record 69 days underground
William Shatnerbecomes the oldest person to reach space, travelling aboard Blue Origin's New Shepard rocket on a 10 minute flight
Simone Bilesbecomes the most decorated gymnast in history when she wins record 25th medal at the World Championships in Stuttgart, Germany
1st military use of trained dolphins (US Navy in Persian Gulf)
24-year-old Rumeysa Gelgi from Turkey confirmed as World's tallest living woman by Guinness World Records at 215.16 centimeters 215.16cm (7ft 0.7in)
Oldest known human remains from Poland at 115,000 years from revealed to be Neanderthal child from Ciemna Cave eaten by a large bird
First electron micrograph of an Ebola viral particle obtained by Dr. F.A. Murphy, working at the C.D.C.
Archaeologists announce the discovery of Arabic characters "Allah” and “Ali” on Viking funeral costumes from a grave in Gamla Uppsala, Sweden
American inventor Garrett Morgan patents his safety hood device, which would later be refined into the gas mask
1st aerial photo taken in US (from a balloon) of Boston
The Whirlpool Galaxy discovered by French astronomerCharles Messier
70,000 people gather to see 'Miracle of the Sun', solar visions reportedly by the Virgin Mary in Fátima, Portugal after prophecies by local children
The term "Beatlemania" is coined after Beatles appear at the London Palladium
IOC Executive Committee approves the reinstatement ofJim Thorpe's gold medals from the 1912 Olympics
Mary Kingsley, English ethnographer and explorer who became the first European to enter parts of Gabon, Africa, born in London (d. 1900)
Mary Hays, American female soldier; Battle of Monmouth, American Revolutionary War (associated with the story of Molly Pitcher), born in Trenton, New Jersey (d. 1832)
Nobel Peace Prize awarded to bankerMuhammad Yunusand Grameen Bank for microlending to the poor
American singer-songwriterBob Dylanis named as recipient of the Nobel Prize for Literature
Kenya's Brigid Kosgei sets new world record of 2:14.04 for the women's marathon in Chicago, beating Paula Radcliffe's 16-year-old mark
Australian swimmerDawn Fraserwins her third consecutive Olympic 100m freestyle gold medal at the Tokyo Games; Olympic record 59.05s
First American Basketball Association (ABA) game is played with Oakland Oaks beating Anaheim Amigos, 132-129; red, white & blue ball and 3-point field goal introduced
Mighty Ducks win their 1st NHL game
Soviet spacecraft Voskhod 1 successfully returns to earth with record three crew
US launches third generation weather satellite, Tiros N
Burglar alarm-ultrasonic or radio waves patented by Samuel Bagno
Revival of Hebrew language as Eliezer Ben-Yehuda & friends agree to use Hebrew exclusively in their conversations
Children's puppet TV program "Junior Jamboree", later renamed "Kukla, Fran & Ollie" premieres locally on WBKB in Chicago, Illinois
IOC announces baseball will become a medal sport in 1992
Edith Sampson (née Spurlock) American lawyer, judge, and the first Black U.S. delegate appointed to the United Nations, born in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania (d. 1979)
Costa Rican President Oscar Arias wins Nobel Peace Prize
Nobel Peace Prize awarded jointly to Polish physicist Joseph Rotblat and the Pugwash Conferences on Science and World Affairs, for their work to reduce and eliminate nuclear weapons
Nobel Prize in Chemistry awarded to Kary Mullis & Michael Smith
Shroud of Turin, revered by many Christians as Christ's burial cloth, is shown by carbon-dating tests to be a fake from the Middle Ages
First US Navy forms when Continental Congress orders construction of a naval fleet
The new gothic style Westminster Abbey is consecrated byHenry IIIafter the bones ofEdward the Confessorare transferred to it. Construction began in 1220.
First Russian Orthodox service in over 70 years held in St Basil's Cathedral, in Red Square, Moscow
1st Baseball World Series: Boston Americans beat Pittsburgh Pirates, 3-0 in Game 8 at Huntington Avenue for a 5-3 series victory
US sweeps medals in the men's 200m backstroke at the Tokyo Olympics; Jed Graef swims world record 2:10.3 to win gold ahead of teammates Gary Dilley and Bob Bennett
NY Jet Nick Lowrey breaks Jan Stenerud NFL field goal record at 374

British Williams driver Damon Hill wins season ending Japanese Grand Prix at Suzuka; becomes first son of a F1 World Drivers Champion (Graham Hill1962, 68) to win title himself; wins by 19 points from Jacques Villeneuve

Michael Schumacherwins season ending Japanese F1 Grand Prix for his record 11th victory of the year & 3rd straight World Drivers Championship; title margin: 67 points from Ferrari teammate Rubens Barrichello
STS 41-G mission; lands at Kennedy Space Center
Jimi HendrixExperience rock trio debuts with American guitarist Jimi Hendrix, British bassist Noel Redding and British drummer Mitch Mitchell in Évreux, Normandy, France
Art Tatum, American jazz pianist and innovator, noted predominantly for his solo work, born in Toledo, Ohio (d. 1956)[1]
Nusrat Fateh Ali Khan, Pakistani qawwal (Sufi devotional music) vocalist, and composer, born in Lyallpur, Pakistan (d. 1997)
Chako Higuchi, Japanese golfer (Women's PGA C'ship 1977; first Asian-born player to win a major title), born in Kawagoe, Saitama, Japan
Burr Tillstrom, American puppeteer (Kukla, Fran & Ollie), born in Chicago, Illinois (d. 1985)
Michael R. Clifford, American astronaut (STS 53, 59, 76), born in Norton AFB, California

Eddie Mathews, American Baseball HOF third baseman (512 career HRs; 12 × MLB All-Star; World Series 1957, 68; Boston/Milwaukee Braves) and manager (Atlanta Braves 1972-74), born in Texarkana, Texas (d. 2001)
Sammy Hagar, American rock vocalist ("I Can't Drive 55"; Van Halen, 1985-96 - "Right Now"), born in Salinas, California
Joey Belladonna [Bellardini], American heavy metal vocalist (Anthrax, 1984-92 - "I Am The Law"), born in Oswego, New York
Robert Lamm, American keyboardist, songwriter, and vocalist (Chicago - "Does Anybody Really Know What Time It Is?": "Beginnings"; "Saturday in the Park"), born in Brooklyn, New York
(Farrell) "Pharoah" Sanders, American jazz saxophonist (John Coltrane groups; "Karma"), born in Little Rock, Arkansas (d. 2022)[1]
Ray Brown, American jazz double bassist (Oscar Peterson; Ella Fitzgerald), born in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania (d. 2002)
Albert Hague [Marcuse], German-American actor, singer and composer (Fame; How the Grinch Stole Christmas), born in Berlin, Germany (d. 2001)
Dorothy Bolden, American civil and women's rights activist, and founder of the National Domestic Worker's Union of America, born in Atlanta, Georgia (d. 2005)
Niè Hǎishèng, Chinese military pilot and CNSA astronaut, born in Zaoyang, Hubei, China
